headline: Stereo vision and layer normalization led a sharp rotation toward 3D reconstruction and rendering techniques this month.
body: Stereo vision and layer normalization both broke out at peak momentum, alongside new entrants in triangle splatting, neural rendering, and primitive-action decomposition that point to rising activity in scene reconstruction and agent action modeling. Local and open-weight models gained ground, with GLM-5.2 surfacing as a free stealth coding model offering roughly 200 requests per five-hour window and Krea 2 arriving as an open-source local image generator with LoRA support. Generative AI improved on the back of new evaluation work, including DiffusionBench for holistic assessment of diffusion transformers. On the agent side, security concerns around prompt injection, data deletion, and unauthorized actions framed slow enterprise adoption, while doer-judge framework and role confusion emerged as new technique entrants.
